name: cable standing reverse grip one arm overhead tricep extension
instructions: Stand facing away from the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold the cable handle with an underhand grip and extend your arm overhead, keeping your elbow close to your head.
Keep your upper arm stationary and slowly lower the cable handle behind your head by bending your elbow.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then extend your arm back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

Stand facing away from the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart. Hold the cable handle with an underhand grip and extend your arm overhead, keeping your elbow close to your head. Keep your upper arm stationary and slowly lower the cable handle behind your head by bending your elbow. Pause for a moment at the bottom, then extend your arm back to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Overhead positions can stress shoulder and elbow joints. Reduce ROM and load as needed.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Avoid pushing into end-range positions. Prioritize midrange control and joint stability over depth.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
